items.title: Thomas Tuchel: “Decepcionado, estuvimos muy cerca pero nos volvimos muy pasivos”
items.summary: Argentina remontó y eliminó a Inglaterra 2-1 en semifinales de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Un resultado que el entrenador Thomas Tuchel lamentó y que, aseguró, fue una derrota merecida por no haber podido recuperar la posesión del balón.

items.title: Argentina remonta a Inglaterra y ya está en la gran final del Mundial | Todo el Mundial
items.summary: Maxi Rodríguez y José Néstor Pekerman analizaron la remontada de Argentina sobre Inglaterra para clasificar a la final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Destacaron que tras el primer gol inglés, tomó el control absoluto del encuentro.

items.title: Maxi Rodríguez: “Inglaterra tuvo miedo de ganar” | Todo el Mundial
items.summary: Nuestro experto analizó el planteamiento del seleccionado inglés y señaló que pudieron aplastar al equipo argentino

items.title: Harry Kane: “Estoy destrozado”
items.summary: El delantero inglés reconoció que la derrota es por demás dolorosa y que se siente triste por todo el trabajo que realizaron.

items.title: Locura argentina en las gradas tras una remontada histórica ante Inglaterra por el pase a la final
items.summary: Los fanáticos ingleses dominaban el partido, pero una reacción agónica de Argentina y un gol de Lautaro Martínez al final desataron la locura albiceleste, dejando devastada a la afición de Inglaterra a un paso de la gran final.

items.title: Giuliano Simeone: “Hay que dar el mensaje de seguir adelante, siempre hay que luchar”
items.summary: Giuliano Simeone declara en conferencia de prensa acerca de la victoria de Argentina sobre Inglaterra en semifinales y agradece a los aficionados por el apoyo y demuestra la felicidad por el buen resultado.

items.title: Fear and hope in Utah in the shadow of America's largest wildfire
items.summary: People in Monticello, Utah have been tense and preparing to evacuate since shortly after the Babylon fire started June 26th

items.title: Democrats’ deepening Israel divisions: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: In today’s edition, our Capitol Hill team dives into the latest disagreement among Democrats over Israel. Plus, Andrea Mitchell examines whether Ukraine can build on the advantages it has developed in the war with Russia.

items.title: Argentina sobrevive a Inglaterra y Messi disputará otra Final del Mundial
items.summary: La Final del Mundial 2026 tendrá sabor a Finalissima tras la remontada de Argentina sobre Inglaterra con goles de Enzo Fernández y Lautaro Martínez.

items.title: Messi festeja frente a una Inglaterra devastada
items.summary: Tras eliminar a Inglaterra en semifinales, Argentina estalla en celebración al avanzar por segunda vez consecutiva a la final. Los ingleses se paran devastados frente a su afición, mientras Messi lidera los festejos albicelestes.

items.title: Drained Reflecting Pool reveals Trump's 'American flag blue' liner is now closer to gray
items.summary: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool's new liner, once dubbed “American flag blue” by President Donald Trump, has faded to a color closer to gray

items.title: Ex-captain pleads guilty to drugging and raping a US Merchant Marine Academy cadet on a cargo ship
items.summary: A former cargo ship captain has pleaded guilty to drugging and raping a 21-year-old U.S. Merchant Marine Academy cadet who was working on the vessel as part of an academy training program

items.title: Vance says Trump administration 'screwed up' communications around Epstein files
items.summary: Vice President JD Vance says the Trump administration “absolutely” mishandled the communications surrounding the Jeffrey Epstein files

items.title: Lautaro Martínez, conmovido tras pasar a la final: “Siempre soñé con hacer este gol”
items.summary: Lautaro Martínez fue una de las figuras de la semifinal donde Argentina venció a Inglaterra, ya que el delantero logró el gol del triunfo en los últimos minutos tras una asistencia de Messi. En sus declaraciones, no pudo evitar llegar hasta las lágrimas.

items.title: Scaloni celebra el pase de Argentina a la final: “Somos únicos”
items.summary: Argentina venció 2-1 a Inglaterra en una histórica semifinal donde remontaron el partido. Al finalizar las acciones, Scaloni se deshizo en elogios para sus jugadores y su afición, destacando el enorme trabajo que han tenido todos para volver a la final.

items.title: Argentina is back in the World Cup final after a thrilling semifinal win over England
items.summary: Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.

items.title: Takeaways from Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing for attorney general
items.summary: Todd Blanche remained composed throughout the hours-long hearing, but faced several testy moments during questioning as he seeks to win the support of all Republican senators on the committee.

items.title: Hair oil and scalp oil are not the same thing — here’s what experts want you to know
items.summary: Hair oil and scalp oil aren’t interchangeable. Hairstylists and trichologists explain the difference, how to use each one and their top product picks.

items.title: ¡Se desata la locura! Lautaro Martínez marca el 2-1 ante Inglaterra
items.summary: El delantero del Inter de Milan le dio la ventaja a la Albiceleste en los últimos minutos para meter a Argentina en la Gran Final.

items.summary: I tested the Thirdlove TempSync T-Shirt Bra for a year to see if it’s actually cooling, quick-drying and helps prevent boob sweat. Here are my honest thoughts, plus sizing tips.

items.title: Muslim man in Utah was targeted in stabbing because of his religion, police say
items.summary: A Utah man told police he repeatedly stabbed a Muslim man because of the man's faith and intended to kill him

items.title: Indian billionaire denies U.S. investment pledge influenced DOJ to seek case dismissal
items.summary: Indian business magnate Gautam Adani said his pledge to invest billions of dollars in the U.S. did not influence the Justice Department’s decision to move to drop the criminal case against him.

items.title: U.S. military will test service members’ testosterone levels, Pete Hegseth says
items.summary: The U.S. military will begin testing the testosterone levels of some service members and recommending hormone therapy if needed, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Wednesday.

items.title: Trump relished in being compared to dictators like Hitler and Stalin, journalist says
items.summary: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

items.title: House Democrats split over U.S. aid to Israel as public opinion shifts
items.summary: More than 100 Democrats voted to cut off U.S. assistance to Israel. The vote also sparked a rare division between the top two House Democrats: Hakeem Jeffries and Katherine Clark.

items.title: US Mint produces $1 coin bearing Trump's face to celebrate America’s 250th birthday
items.summary: The U.S. Mint has started producing a new $1 coin featuring President Donald Trump to help celebrate America’s 250th birthday

items.title: Amazon’s secret sale on air conditioners and fans can help you beat the heat
items.summary: Amazon has hidden deals on window air conditioners, portable AC units and personal fans from Shark, LG and more, with prices up to 35% off.

items.title: Blanche questioned on Epstein files, crime and IRS settlement in confirmation hearing
items.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche faced questions from senators on the handling of the Epstein files, crime statistics and a Trump settlement with the IRS during his confirmation hearing to permanently serve in the position. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin reports from Capitol Hill on what lawmakers highlighted in their questioning.

items.title: Fed chair Warsh sidesteps Senate questions on inflation, AI, contact with Trump
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh on Wednesday sought to evade a range of questions from senators on issues such as the impact of AI on inflation, what contacts he has had with President Donald Trump, and how the central bank will determine the persist...

items.title: Federal government installs reworked panels about slavery at George Washington's Philadelphia home

items.title: Cancer disparities researchers say federal funding changes have disrupted their work
items.summary: In a survey, 93% of cancer researchers who study disparities said federal policy changes have affected them. Funding is harder to come by and they worry it's slowing progress in their field.

items.summary: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Greetings from Paris, where an art installation transformed the Pont Neuf into a rocky cave
items.summary: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.

items.title: Wildfires expose millions in the Midwest and Northeast US to dangerous smoke
items.summary: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week, exposing millions of people to dangerous air pollution

items.title: Federal appeals court ends a decades-old school desegregation order in Louisiana
items.summary: A federal appeals court has ended more than 60 years of federal oversight of a Louisiana school system that had been ordered to eradicate segregation

items.title: 1 missing and 17 rescued after a boat fire near Alcatraz Island off San Francisco
items.summary: Authorities in San Francisco say one person has died, three people are missing and 16 others have been rescued from waters off the city after a pontoon boat sank

items.title: Texas drenched by heavy rains as forecasters warn more storms could bring floods
items.summary: Heavy downpours in South Texas washed out highways and stranded motorists Tuesday as forecasters warned that a threat of more severe weather could bring dangerous flooding to already drenched counties near the border with Mexico

items.title: March for democracy planned for anniversary of historic MLK-led 1963 Washington march
items.summary: A large coalition led by the Rev. Al Sharpton and the family of the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. will mark the anniversary of the historic March on Washington next month with a march for democracy

items.title: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history
items.summary: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: Beef tallow as moisturizer? I asked derms if this trend is genius or gross
items.summary: Is beef tallow a good moisturizer? Dermatologists break down the benefits, risks, and whether this viral skincare trend is worth trying.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
